key_points: For a long time, many societies believed that educating girls was unnecessary
Girls were expected to stay at home and learn household work instead of going to school
Social reformers in the 19th century began campaigning for girls' education
Education helped women gain awareness of their rights and opportunities
Today women contribute actively in fields like politics, science, medicine, education, and business
examples: Savitribai Phule and Jyotirao Phule opened the first school for girls in Pune
Rashsundari Devi secretly learned to read and later wrote the autobiography 'Amar Jiban'
Many girls today attend school and pursue higher education
Women scientists, athletes, and leaders inspire young girls across the world
Self-help groups enable rural women to start businesses and support their families
formulas: Access to Education → Improved Quality of Life
exam_tips: Understand the historical barriers to girls' education
Remember important reformers like Savitribai Phule
Learn examples of women overcoming social restrictions
Focus on how education contributes to women's empowerment
